workshoptitle: One Restaurant, Every Model: From Document Pain to Converged Resolution

tutorials.title: Get Started
tutorials.description: Sign up for a free trial or use your own Oracle Cloud tenancy.

tutorials.title: Introduction
tutorials.description: Multi-model is a storage claim; converged is a guarantees claim. What you will build and why.

tutorials.title: Lab 1: Set Up Your Workspace and Run the Preflight
tutorials.description: Open Cloud Shell and Database Actions, connect mongosh, and validate every dependency of the later labs.

tutorials.title: Lab 2: Ship It Like It's 2015 — The Embedded Menu (MongoDB API)
tutorials.description: Build the classic embedded document model with unchanged mongosh against Autonomous Database.

tutorials.title: Lab 3: Corporate Changes One Price — The Bet Inverts
tutorials.description: Feel the embedding pattern fail: fleet-wide rewrites, $unwind analytics, and silent type drift.

tutorials.title: Lab 4: Model the Domain — One Truth (Canonical Schema + SQL/JSON)
tutorials.description: Discover your collection is already a SQL table, then shred it into the canonical restaurant schema.

tutorials.title: Lab 5: Get the Document Back — Duality Views and Governance
tutorials.description: Recover the document shape from canonical rows, prove one enforcement domain, and read it through REST.

tutorials.title: Lab 6: The Third Door, Writable — REST ETag Choreography (Optional)
tutorials.description: Lock-free optimistic concurrency through ORDS with zero application code.

tutorials.title: Lab 7: Orders and the Upsell Graph (SQL/PGQ)
tutorials.description: Store orders as native documents, then project a property graph and answer 'do you want fries with that?'

tutorials.title: Lab 8: Meaning, Not Keywords — and the One-Statement Finale
tutorials.description: In-database ONNX vector search over the same tables, then graph + relational + vector in one optimizer plan.

tutorials.title: Need Help?
tutorials.description: Solutions to Common Problems and Directions for Receiving Live Help
